seoTitle: Vicor Products | DC-DC Converters, VI Chip Modules, ChiP Power Modules
seoDescription: Browse Vicor's comprehensive power component portfolio including DC-DC converters, VI Chip modules, ChiP power modules, and power systems for data center, industrial, automotive, and aerospace applications.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Vicor DC-DC converter for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Vicor DC-DC converter involves: 1) Input voltage range - ensure converter input rating covers your system voltage; 2) Output voltage - select fixed or adjustable output as needed; 3) Output power - choose converter with adequate power rating (typically use 50-80% of rated power); 4) Efficiency requirements - Vicor offers up to 98% efficiency; 5) Isolation requirements - isolated vs non-isolated designs. Vicor provides online design tools and simulation to help with selection. Contact our FAE team for application-specific recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our product selector or contact FAE for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between isolated and non-isolated DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Key differences between isolated and non-isolated converters: Isolated converters: Provide electrical isolation between input and output using transformers; Offer higher input/output voltage ratios; Better common-mode noise rejection; Required for applications needing ground loop isolation. Non-isolated converters: Direct connection between input and output; Higher efficiency due to fewer components; More compact and lower cost; Suitable when isolation is not required. Vicor offers both types across their product portfolio. Choice depends on your system requirements for isolation, voltage ratios, and efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for guidance on isolated vs non-isolated selection for your application.

faqs.question: What packages are available for Vicor modules?
faqs.answer: Vicor modules are available in various packages: ChiP packages - SM-ChiP (surface mount) from 9mm to 23mm height, DIP (through-hole) packages for high power; VI Chip packages - compact modules for Factorized Power Architecture; Evaluation boards - development platforms for rapid prototyping. Package selection depends on: Power level - higher power requires larger packages; Thermal management - thermal pad vs heat sink options; Assembly method - SMD vs through-hole; Form factor constraints. Vicor provides thermal and mechanical specifications for each package.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for package recommendations based on your power and thermal requirements.

faqs.question: What is Factorized Power Architecture (FPA) and when should I use it?
faqs.answer: Factorized Power Architecture (FPA) separates power conversion into two specialized functions: PRM (Pronto Module) for voltage regulation and VTM (Voltage Transformation Module) for current multiplication. FPA advantages include: Higher system efficiency (up to 97% per stage); Better thermal management with distributed heat sources; Flexible scaling through parallel operation; Dynamic voltage positioning for load optimization. Use FPA when: High current is required at point-of-load; System efficiency is critical; Thermal management is challenging; Load requirements may change. Contact our FAE team for FPA design guidance and component selection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for FPA architecture assessment and component selection for your power system.

faqs.question: How do I get pricing and availability for Vicor products?
faqs.answer: As an authorized Vicor distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides competitive pricing and stock availability for all Vicor products. To get pricing: Contact our sales team with your part numbers and quantities; Request volume pricing for production quantities; Ask about scheduled delivery programs for long-term supply. For availability: Check current stock for standard products; Lead times for non-stock items typically 4-8 weeks; Expedite options available for urgent requirements. Technical support and samples are also available through our FAE team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for current pricing, availability, and to discuss your procurement requirements.

categories.name: DC-DC Converters
categories.description: Vicor's DC-DC converters deliver industry-leading power density and efficiency for a wide range of applications from 1W to 2kW.
categories.longDescription: Vicor's DC-DC converter portfolio represents the state-of-the-art in power conversion technology available through authorized distributors. These high-performance converters feature efficiencies up to 98%, power densities up to 1,000W per cubic inch, and exceptional reliability. The product range spans from compact 1W modules for portable applications to robust 2kW converters for industrial systems. Vicor DC-DC converters incorporate advanced thermal management technologies including proprietary thermal pads and ChiP packaging that enable direct heatsinking without external hardware. Available in both isolated and non-isolated topologies, these converters support a wide range of input and output voltages including 24V, 48V, 72V, and 380V nominal inputs common in data center, automotive, and industrial applications. Our distributor selection guide helps you choose the right converter series for your application requirements. All modules are factory-tested and backed by Vicor's reputation for quality and reliability.

series.name: Maxi Series
series.description: High-power DC-DC converters up to 600W with excellent thermal performance

series.name: Mini Series
series.description: Compact DC-DC converters from 50W to 150W for space-constrained applications

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Vicor DC-DC Converters
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting DC-DC converters based on voltage, power, and application requirements

faqs.question: What is the typical efficiency of Vicor DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Vicor DC-DC converters offer industry-leading efficiency: Typical efficiency ranges from 91% to 98% depending on model and operating conditions; Peak efficiency often occurs at 50-75% load; Full-load efficiency typically 1-2% below peak. Factors affecting efficiency: Input voltage - efficiency varies with input voltage ratio; Load current - efficiency typically peaks at moderate loads; Temperature - efficiency may decrease at temperature extremes. Vicor provides detailed efficiency curves in datasheets. For applications requiring maximum efficiency, consider selecting converters sized for 50-80% of rated load.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for efficiency comparisons and selection based on your load profile.

faqs.question: How do I parallel Vicor DC-DC converters for higher power?
faqs.answer: Parallel operation of Vicor DC-DC converters: Most Vicor converters support parallel operation for redundancy or increased power; Use identical models from same production batch for best current sharing; Enable droop sharing or active current sharing as specified; Maintain adequate spacing between converters for thermal management. Design considerations: Calculate total power requirement and select converter quantity; Use proper bus bars or PCB copper for current distribution; Implement paralleling diodes if isolation is needed; Consider using a power shelf or custom bus bar assembly. Vicor provides paralleling guidelines and reference designs for high-power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for paralleling guidelines and system design recommendations.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for Vicor converters?
faqs.answer: Vicor converter thermal management: ChiP modules feature integrated thermal pads for direct heatsinking; Use thermal interface material between module and heatsink; Calculate power dissipation: P_loss = P_out x (1/η - 1); Ensure adequate airflow or heatsink capacity. Options: Natural convection heatsinks for low-power applications; Forced air cooling for higher power; Liquid cooling plates for demanding thermal environments; Direct mounting to system chassis as heatsink. Vicor provides thermal models and simulation tools for accurate thermal design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal design support and heatsink recommendations.

faqs.question: Can Vicor converters operate in harsh environments?
faqs.answer: Vicor converters are designed for various environmental conditions: Standard models rated for 0°C to +85°C or +125°C operation; Extended temperature models available up to +125°C or higher; High-reliability models for industrial and defense applications; Custom solutions for extreme environments. Environmental ratings: Operating temperature range; Storage temperature; Humidity rating (typically 95% RH non-condensing); Vibration and shock resistance; EMI/EMC compliance. For harsh environments, specify high-reliability or custom models with appropriate environmental ratings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for environmental specifications and harsh environment recommendations.

faqs.question: What control and monitoring features do Vicor converters offer?
faqs.answer: Vicor converter control and monitoring features: On/Off control - primary or secondary side enable; Output voltage trim - adjust output within specified range; Current sharing - parallel operation control; Status indicators - output good, fault detection; PMBus interface - digital communication and monitoring on select models. Optional features: Remote sense - compensation for voltage drop; Tracking - synchronize startup/shutdown sequence; Margin programming - adjust output for testing. Feature availability varies by model. Check datasheet for specific capabilities.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for control feature availability and implementation guidance.

products.name: 24V Input 400W Isolated DC-DC Converter
products.shortDescription: 400W isolated DC-DC converter with 24V input and adjustable 12V output for industrial and data center applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The V24A12T400BN is a 400W isolated DC-DC converter with 24V nominal input voltage.
Features adjustable output voltage from 9.6V to 15V with tight regulation and low output noise.
High-efficiency design up to 92% with excellent thermal performance in ChiP package.
features: High power density up to 730W per cubic inch
Integrated thermal pad for direct heatsinking
Output voltage trim and remote sense

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The V24A12T400BN is my go-to recommendation for 24V industrial power applications. I've specified this converter in numerous factory automation and industrial control systems. The 92% efficiency is excellent for isolated designs, and the integrated thermal pad makes thermal design straightforward. In a recent PLC control system, this converter delivered 400W in a compact form factor that fit perfectly in the existing enclosure. The trim and remote sense features are valuable for compensating voltage drops in distributed power architectures. The parallel capability is useful for applications requiring higher power or redundancy.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent choice for 24V industrial applications with outstanding power density

alternativeParts.comparison: V24A12T400BN=><V24A8T300BN: Efficiency 91% < 92% (slightly lower), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Lower power option for applications requiring 8V output
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for lower power applications or different output voltage requirements

alternativeParts.comparison: V24A12T400BN=><V48A12T500BN: Efficiency 93% > 92% (higher), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Higher power option for 48V input applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for 48V bus systems requiring higher power output

companionParts.description: Thermal interface pad for ChiP thermal pad

faqs.question: What is the input voltage range of V24A12T400BN?
faqs.answer: The V24A12T400BN input voltage specifications: Nominal input: 24V DC; Operating range: 18V to 36V DC; Absolute maximum: 50V DC (survival without damage); Transient capability: 50V for 100ms. The converter maintains regulation and protection across the full operating range. For 24V battery systems, ensure the battery voltage during charging (up to 29V for Li-ion) stays within operating range. For automotive 24V systems, account for load dump transients and use appropriate TVS protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for input transient protection recommendations and system design guidance.

faqs.question: How do I mount V24A12T400BN to a heatsink?
faqs.answer: Mounting procedure for V24A12T400BN: Apply thermal interface material (TIM) to the thermal pad area on bottom of module; Place module on heatsink with thermal pad aligned; Secure with M3 or similar screws per recommended torque (0.56-0.79 Nm); Ensure flatness of mounting surface < 0.1mm. Important notes: Don't overtighten screws - can damage module; Use spring washers to maintain pressure over thermal cycles; Allow TIM to spread during first thermal cycles; Consider using thermal compound instead of pads for maximum performance. Vicor provides detailed mounting instructions in the product datasheet.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for mounting guidelines and thermal interface recommendations.

faqs.question: Can I adjust the output voltage of V24A12T400BN?
faqs.answer: Output voltage adjustment for V24A12T400BN: Trim range: 9.6V to 15V DC; Trim method: External resistor connected to TRIM pin; Calculation: Vicor provides trim resistor formulas in datasheet; Remote sense: Compensates for voltage drop in output connections (up to 0.5V). Trim resistor connection: Connect resistor from TRIM pin to output return; Calculate value using Vicor trim equations; Accuracy: Output regulation maintained within specified tolerance across trim range. For precise output voltage, use the trim feature and verify with accurate measurement during setup.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for trim resistor calculation assistance and output voltage setting guidance.

faqs.question: What protection features does V24A12T400BN include?
faqs.answer: Protection features of V24A12T400BN: Input overvoltage lockout - shuts down if input exceeds ~38V; Output overvoltage protection - limits output if regulation fails; Output overcurrent protection - constant current limiting at ~125% rated current; Overtemperature protection - thermal shutdown at ~125°C case temperature; Undervoltage lockout - prevents operation if input drops below ~16V. Protection behavior: Most faults result in hiccup mode operation with automatic restart; Thermal shutdown requires cool-down before restart; Latched protection on some fault conditions. These protections ensure reliable operation in demanding environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for protection behavior details and application-specific protection requirements.

faqs.question: How efficient is V24A12T400BN at partial load?
faqs.answer: V24A12T400BN efficiency characteristics: Peak efficiency: ~92% at 50-75% load; Full load efficiency: ~91.5%; Half load efficiency: ~90.5%; Quarter load efficiency: ~87%. For optimal efficiency: Size converter for 50-80% of rated load; Consider lower-power model if load is consistently light; Use multiple smaller converters if load varies significantly. Vicor efficiency curves show detailed performance vs load. At typical industrial loads (60-80%), efficiency remains above 91%. For battery-powered applications with variable load, efficiency at low load becomes important.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for efficiency analysis based on your specific load profile.

products.name: 48V Input 500W Isolated DC-DC Converter
products.shortDescription: 500W isolated DC-DC converter with 48V input and 24V output, optimized for 48V data center and telecom applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The V48A24T500BL is a 500W isolated DC-DC converter designed for 48V power distribution systems.
Provides regulated 24V output from 36-55V input with high efficiency up to 93%.
Optimized for 48V bus architectures common in data centers, telecom, and battery-backed systems.
features: 93% peak efficiency for reduced power loss
Integrated thermal pad for heatsink mounting
PMBus compatible for monitoring and control

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Data Center Power
faeReview.content: The V48A24T500BL is specifically designed for 48V power architectures, which are increasingly popular in data centers due to efficiency advantages. I've worked on several 48V data center designs where this converter played a key role. The 93% efficiency is excellent for this power level, and the low-profile package is valuable for blade server applications where board real estate is premium. The PMBus compatibility is a major advantage for intelligent power management - we can monitor voltage, current, and temperature, and even control output on/off remotely. For 48V bus systems, this is my top recommendation for 24V distribution.
faeReview.highlight: Purpose-built for 48V data center architectures with PMBus intelligence

alternativeParts.comparison: V48A24T500BL=><V48A12T500BL: Efficiency 93% = 93% (same), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Same power with 12V output for different load requirements

alternativeParts.comparison: V48A24T500BL=><V48C24T300BL: Efficiency 93.5% > 93% (slightly higher), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Lower power but higher efficiency for lighter loads
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for lower power 48V applications where efficiency is critical

companionParts.description: USB to PMBus adapter for converter programming

faqs.question: What are the advantages of 48V power distribution?
faqs.answer: 48V power distribution advantages: Efficiency: Lower I2R losses compared to 12V for same power; Cable size: Smaller conductors needed for same power; Safety: 48V is considered safe voltage (no special safety training required); Standardization: 48V widely used in telecom and data centers; Ecosystem: Large ecosystem of 48V products and components. Comparison: 48V vs 12V for 5kW: At 12V, current is 417A requiring large cables; At 48V, current is 104A with significantly smaller cables; Power loss at same cable resistance is 16x lower at 48V. For high-power applications, 48V bus significantly reduces distribution losses and costs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for power architecture analysis comparing 48V vs other bus voltages.

faqs.question: How do I program the V48A24T500BL using PMBus?
faqs.answer: PMBus programming for V48A24T500BL: Connect PMBus adapter to converter's PMBus pins; Use Vicor software tools or standard PMBus commands; Programmable parameters: Output voltage trim; Turn-on/off delays; Fault response; Output voltage Margining; Monitored values: Output voltage, current, temperature; Input voltage status. PMBus address: Set using address pins or programming; Default address varies by model. Vicor provides comprehensive PMBus documentation and example code. The PMBus interface enables sophisticated power management and system integration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for PMBus programming guide and software tool access.

faqs.question: Can I use V48A24T500BL in parallel for redundancy?
faqs.answer: Parallel operation of V48A24T500BL: Supported for both increased power and N+1 redundancy; Enable droop load sharing between paralleled units; Use same model from same production batch for best matching; Proper layout critical for current sharing. Redundancy design: N+1 configuration - one extra converter for fault tolerance; ORing diodes or controllers for bus isolation; Total load should not exceed N converters at full load; Parallel controllers available for sophisticated redundancy. Vicor provides paralleling guidelines for this converter series.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for paralleling guidelines and redundancy architecture recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the hold-up time requirement for V48A24T500BL?
faqs.answer: Hold-up time considerations: Hold-up time - duration converter maintains output during input interruption; V48A24T500BL typical hold-up: ~10ms at full load with standard input capacitance; Extended hold-up: Add input capacitors per Vicor guidelines. For critical applications: Calculate energy storage needed: Energy (J) = P_out x hold-up_time / efficiency; Add film capacitors at converter input; Consider backup battery for extended hold-up. Standards: Some standards require specific hold-up times (e.g., 20ms for IT equipment). Vicor provides formulas for calculating required capacitance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for hold-up time calculations and capacitor selection guidance.

faqs.question: What EMI performance does V48A24T500BL meet?
faqs.answer: EMI performance of V48A24T500BL: Meets EN 55032 Class B and FCC Part 15 Class B with appropriate filtering; Conducted emissions: External EMI filter recommended for strict compliance; Radiated emissions: Shielding may be needed for sensitive applications. EMC design tips: Use recommended input/output filter components; Proper grounding of chassis and signal returns; PCB layout guidelines in datasheet; Ferrite beads on cables may help. Vicor provides EMI filter recommendations and layout guidelines. For pre-compliance testing, contact our FAE team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for EMI filter recommendations and pre-compliance testing support.

categories.name: VI Chip Modules
categories.description: Vicor's VI Chip modules including VTM current multipliers and PRM voltage regulators enable the revolutionary Factorized Power Architecture.
categories.longDescription: Vicor's VI Chip modules are the building blocks of the Factorized Power Architecture (FPA), a revolutionary approach to power system design available through authorized distributors. These modules separate power conversion into specialized functions for optimal performance. The VTM (Voltage Transformation Module) is a high-efficiency, high-density current multiplier that provides isolated DC-DC conversion with exceptional current handling capability. Operating at high switching frequencies with proprietary zero-voltage switching technology, VTMs achieve efficiencies up to 97%. The PRM (Pronto Module) is a high-performance voltage regulator module that provides dynamic bus voltage control, enabling optimization of power delivery based on load requirements. Our distributor selection guide helps you choose the right VI Chip series for your FPA implementation. Together, PRM and VTM modules enable power architectures with higher efficiency, better thermal management, and more flexible scalability than traditional approaches.

series.name: VTM Series
series.description: Current multiplier modules for isolated high-current DC-DC conversion

series.name: PRM Series
series.description: Voltage regulator modules for dynamic bus voltage control

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Vicor VI Chip Modules for FPA
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting VI Chip modules and designing Factorized Power Architecture systems

faqs.question: How does Factorized Power Architecture work?
faqs.answer: Factorized Power Architecture (FPA) works by separating power conversion into specialized functions: PRM (Pronto Module) - acts as a voltage regulator providing dynamic bus voltage (typically 48V); VTM (Voltage Transformation Module) - acts as a current multiplier providing isolated DC-DC conversion with high current output. The PRM optimizes power processing for regulation while the VTM handles voltage transformation and isolation with high efficiency. This separation allows each function to operate at its optimal point, enabling higher overall system efficiency and power density.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for FPA design guidance and component selection.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of FPA over traditional power architecture?
faqs.answer: FPA advantages over traditional approaches: Higher efficiency - each function optimized independently; Better thermal management - heat sources localized and managed separately; Flexible scaling - add parallel VTM modules for more current; Dynamic voltage positioning - PRM can adjust bus voltage based on load; Reduced distribution losses - high-current conversion happens close to load. Traditional vs FPA for 1000A processor power: Traditional: 48V to 1V in one converter with large distribution current; FPA: 48V to 48V PRM + 48V to 1V VTM at load with lower distribution current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for detailed FPA vs traditional architecture comparison for your application.

faqs.question: How do I size PRM and VTM modules for my application?
faqs.answer: Sizing FPA modules: PRM selection: Based on total system power requirement; Consider 80% loading for reliability; PRM provides regulation and bus control. VTM selection: Based on load voltage and current requirements; VTMs available for various input/output ratios; Multiple VTMs can parallel for higher current. Example sizing for 1000A at 1V from 48V bus: PRM: P048F4802T512 (5kW module); VTM: NBM2317 (40A module), need 25 modules for 1000A. Vicor provides online sizing tools and FAE support for system design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for FPA system sizing and component selection.

faqs.question: Can I parallel VI Chip modules?
faqs.answer: Parallel operation of VI Chip modules: VTM modules support parallel operation for higher current; Use droop current sharing for load balancing; Parallel all units to common input and output buses. PRM modules support parallel operation for higher power; Enable auto-crossover for load sharing; Synchronized turn-on/off for system coordination. Design considerations: Use identical modules from same batch for best matching; Maintain symmetry in bus bar layout; Calculate bus bar requirements for current capacity; Implement proper fusing for fault isolation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for paralleling guidelines and bus bar design recommendations.

faqs.question: What thermal management is needed for VI Chip modules?
faqs.answer: VI Chip thermal management: VTM modules: High power density requires effective thermal management; Integrated thermal pads for direct heatsinking; Water cooling options available for high-power applications. PRM modules: Moderate power density with flexible thermal options; Heatsink or cold plate mounting. Design approach: Calculate power dissipation in each module; Determine heatsink thermal resistance required; Select appropriate cooling method; Consider liquid cooling for high-power systems. Vicor provides thermal models and simulation tools for accurate thermal design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal design support and cooling recommendations.

products.name: 48V Input 200A VTM Current Multiplier
products.shortDescription: 200A high-efficiency current multiplier for FPA, converting 48V to 1V at 200A with 97% peak efficiency.
descriptionParagraphs: The VTM48EF040T200A00 is a 200A VTM current multiplier for Factorized Power Architecture.
Converts 48V input to 1V output at 200A continuous with industry-leading 97% efficiency.
Designed for powering high-current loads like GPUs, FPGAs, and ASICs directly at the point of load.
features: 97% peak efficiency for reduced power loss
High current density - 200A from compact module
Integrated thermal interface for direct cooling
Low output impedance for excellent transient response

faeReview.author: John Zhang
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - High-Power Systems
faeReview.content: The VTM48EF040T200A00 is a game-changer for high-current power delivery. I've designed power systems for AI accelerators and GPU clusters where traditional approaches struggled with distribution losses and thermal management. With this VTM, the 48V bus comes directly to the GPU board, and the VTM provides 200A at 1V right at the load. The 97% efficiency means minimal heat generation, and the thermal pad allows direct attachment to the board's heatsink. In a recent AI accelerator design, we achieved 15% higher efficiency compared to the previous design using conventional converters. For any high-current application, this VTM is the answer.
faeReview.highlight: Enables unprecedented power delivery for AI accelerators and high-current processors

alternativeParts.comparison: VTM48EF040T200A00=><VTM48EF040T100A00: Output current 100A < 200A (half), Efficiency 97% = 97% (same), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Lower current option for lighter loads
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for lower current processors or to create balanced parallel configurations

alternativeParts.reason: Higher output voltage ratio for different processor voltages

Input/output bus bars for VTM paralleling

faqs.question: How do I parallel VTM48EF040T200A00 modules?
faqs.answer: Paralleling VTM modules for higher current: Enable droop load sharing between parallel units; Use identical modules from same production batch; Connect all inputs in parallel to common bus; Connect all outputs in parallel to common output bus. Bus bar design: Use low-impedance bus bars sized for total current; Maintain symmetry in connections; Calculate resistance for <1mV difference between modules. Example: For 800A with four 200A modules: Each module sees approximately 200A; Use 2mΩ bus resistance for 1mV droop at 500A. Vicor provides paralleling guidelines and bus bar calculators.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for paralleling guidelines and bus bar design assistance.

faqs.question: What is the thermal performance of VTM48EF040T200A00?
faqs.answer: Thermal performance of VTM48EF040T200A00: Power dissipation at 200A, 1V out, 97% efficiency: P_loss = 200W x (1/0.97 - 1) = 6.2W; Thermal resistance: Junction to thermal pad ~0.5°C/W; Max case temperature: Limited by internal components; Operating range: -40°C to +125°C case temperature. Cooling options: Air cooling with heatsink for lower power; Liquid cooling cold plate for full power; Direct attachment to board heatsink. Thermal design: Calculate required thermal resistance; Ensure adequate cooling capacity; Monitor case temperature in system.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for thermal design support and cooling recommendations.

faqs.question: How does VTM efficiency compare to traditional converters?
faqs.answer: VTM vs traditional converter efficiency: VTM efficiency: Up to 97% peak; Traditional 48V-1V converter: Typically 88-92% efficiency; Improvement: 5-9 percentage points higher with VTM. Why VTM is more efficient: Fixed-ratio transformation is simpler than regulation; Zero-voltage switching minimizes switching losses; High-frequency operation with optimized magnetics; No need for output filtering inductors. System-level impact: For 100A at 1V: Traditional converter loss = 12W; VTM loss = 3W; 75% reduction in losses means significantly less cooling required.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for efficiency comparison and system-level analysis for your application.

faqs.question: What is the startup behavior of VTM48EF040T200A00?
faqs.answer: VTM startup characteristics: Soft-start: Configurable rise time typically 1-5ms; Pre-bias startup: Can start with voltage present on output; Inrush current: Limited by internal circuitry; Turn-on delay: Typically 5-10ms after enable. Startup sequence: Enable signal received; Internal circuits initialize; Output voltage ramps with soft-start; Full current available after soft-start complete. Pre-bias capability: VTM can start into capacitive loads; Output follows input ratio during soft-start; No damage from pre-existing voltage on output. This enables reliable startup in complex systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for startup timing configuration and system startup sequencing guidance.

faqs.question: Can VTM modules be used without a PRM?
faqs.answer: Using VTM without PRM: Yes, VTM modules can operate directly from a 48V bus; Input voltage range: 38-55V for standard VTMs; Output follows input ratio (e.g., 48V in = 1V out at 1/48 ratio). Limitations without PRM: No output voltage regulation - output varies with input; No dynamic voltage positioning; Full system functionality limited. When to use PRM: When output voltage regulation is required; For dynamic voltage scaling; To optimize efficiency across load range. For applications where input voltage is stable and fixed ratio is acceptable, direct VTM operation can be cost-effective.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for guidance on using VTM with or without PRM based on your requirements.

products.name: 48V 200W PRM Voltage Regulator Module
products.shortDescription: 200W PRM voltage regulator for FPA, providing regulated output from 38-55V input with dynamic voltage control.
descriptionParagraphs: The PRM48NF480T200A00 is a 200W PRM voltage regulator module for Factorized Power Architecture.
Provides regulated output voltage from 48V input with high efficiency and dynamic control capability.
Enables FPA with PRM handling regulation while VTM handles current multiplication.

faeReview.author: Sarah Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Architecture
faeReview.content: The PRM is the intelligence in Factorized Power Architecture. In a recent system design for a high-performance computing platform, we used PRM modules to create a regulated 48V bus that supplied multiple VTMs for point-of-load conversion. The PMBus interface was invaluable - we could monitor power consumption, adjust bus voltage based on workload, and implement sophisticated power management. The dynamic voltage scaling capability allows reducing bus voltage during low-load periods to improve system efficiency. For anyone building advanced power systems, the PRM/VTM combination is the way to go.
faeReview.highlight: Enables intelligent power management with dynamic voltage scaling in FPA systems

alternativeParts.reason: Higher power option for larger systems

faqs.question: What is dynamic voltage scaling with PRM?
faqs.answer: Dynamic voltage scaling (DVS) with PRM: PRM can adjust output voltage dynamically based on control signals; Typical range: 20-100% of rated output voltage; Update rate: Can change voltage in < 1ms; Use cases: Optimize power consumption based on workload; Reduce voltage during idle periods; Implement power saving modes. Implementation: PMBus commands for voltage changes; Analog control voltage input; Predefined voltage profiles. Benefits: 10-30% power savings in variable load applications; Reduced cooling requirements; Extended component life from lower thermal stress.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for DVS implementation guidance and power savings analysis.

faqs.question: How do I program PRM modules using PMBus?
faqs.answer: PMBus programming for PRM modules: Connect PMBus adapter to PRM communication pins; Use Vicor software tools or standard PMBus commands; Programmable parameters: Output voltage setpoint; Fault thresholds and responses; Turn-on/off delays; Enable/disable control. Monitored parameters: Input/output voltage and current; Module temperature; Fault status; Operating hours. PMBus features: Address configuration via pins or programming; Telemetry logging for diagnostics; Firmware updates on select models. Vicor provides comprehensive PMBus documentation and example code.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for PMBus programming guide and software tool access.

faqs.question: Can PRM modules be paralleled?
faqs.answer: Parallel operation of PRM modules: Supported for higher power and redundancy; Auto-crossover feature for automatic load sharing; Synchronized operation for system coordination. Paralleling requirements: Use identical PRM models; Configure matching parameters via PMBus; Proper input/output bus distribution; Communication bus for coordination. Redundancy: N+1 configuration for fault tolerance; ORing protection for bus isolation; Load sharing ensures equal stress. Vicor provides paralleling guidelines and reference designs for multi-module systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for PRM paralleling guidelines and system architecture recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the hold-up time for PRM modules?
faqs.answer: PRM hold-up time characteristics: Hold-up time depends on input capacitance and load; Typical internal capacitance provides ~1-2ms hold-up; External capacitors can extend hold-up as needed. Calculating hold-up requirements: Determine maximum acceptable output voltage dip during input interruption; Calculate required energy storage; Add film capacitors at PRM input. Example: For 100ms hold-up at 200W: Energy needed = 200W x 0.1s = 20J; At 48V, capacitance = 2 x 20J / (48V)^2 = 17,000uF. Vicor provides formulas and guidelines for hold-up capacitor selection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for hold-up time calculations and capacitor selection guidance.

faqs.question: How does PRM work with VTM in FPA?
faqs.answer: PRM and VTM interaction in FPA: PRM provides: Input filtering and surge protection; Output voltage regulation (the dynamic bus voltage); System monitoring and control via PMBus. VTM provides: Fixed-ratio transformation from PRM output; High-current multiplication; Isolation if required. System operation: PRM regulates 48V bus; VTMs draw from 48V bus and transform to load voltage; VTM output follows PRM voltage ratio; System efficiency = PRM_eff x VTM_eff. Benefits: Each module optimized for its function; Flexible scaling with parallel modules; Dynamic voltage positioning for power optimization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for FPA design guidance and system architecture recommendations.

categories.name: ChiP Power Modules
categories.description: Vicor's ChiP modules offer the highest power density in compact surface-mount packages for space-constrained applications.
categories.longDescription: Vicor's ChiP (Converter housed in Package) modules represent the pinnacle of power module packaging technology available through authorized distributors. These innovative modules house complete power converters in thermally-optimized packages that enable unprecedented power densities. The SM-ChiP (Surface Mount ChiP) series offers heights from 9mm to 23mm, allowing design flexibility for ultra-thin applications. ChiP modules feature integrated thermal pads that enable direct attachment to heatsinks or system chassis, eliminating bulky heat sinks and improving thermal performance. Available in both isolated and non-isolated topologies, ChiP modules cover power ranges from 50W to over 1kW with efficiencies up to 98%. Our distributor selection guide helps you choose the right ChiP series for your power density and thermal requirements. The surface-mount form factor enables automated assembly for high-volume manufacturing while the robust construction ensures reliability in demanding environments.

series.name: SM-ChiP Series
series.description: Surface mount ChiP modules from 9mm to 23mm height

series.name: DChiP Series
series.description: Through-hole ChiP modules for higher power applications

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Vicor ChiP Modules
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting ChiP modules based on power, thermal, and form factor requirements

faqs.question: What are the advantages of ChiP modules?
faqs.answer: ChiP module advantages: Highest power density - up to 1,000W per cubic inch; Integrated thermal pad - direct heatsink attachment without hardware; Surface mount assembly - compatible with standard SMT manufacturing; Flexible thermal options - air, forced air, or liquid cooling; High efficiency - up to 98% peak efficiency; Robust construction - proven reliability in demanding environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for ChiP module recommendations for your application.

faqs.question: How do I select between SM-ChiP and DChiP packages?
faqs.answer: Package selection criteria: SM-ChiP: Surface mount for automated assembly; Lower profile options (9mm-23mm); Best for high-volume production; Ideal for thin systems. DChiP: Through-hole for higher power; Better thermal performance; Easier manual assembly; Higher current ratings. Choose based on: Assembly method (SMT vs through-hole); Power level required; Form factor constraints; Volume requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for package selection guidance based on your assembly and power requirements.

faqs.question: What thermal interface is required for ChiP modules?
faqs.answer: ChiP thermal interface requirements: Integrated thermal pad on bottom of module; Thermal interface material (TIM) required between pad and heatsink; Options: Thermal grease, thermal pads, phase change materials. Selection criteria: Power dissipation level; Thermal resistance requirements; Assembly method; Reliability requirements. Vicor provides thermal models and TIM recommendations for each module.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for thermal interface material selection and thermal design guidance.

faqs.question: Can ChiP modules be used in high-reliability applications?
faqs.answer: High-reliability ChiP applications: Vicor offers Hi-Rel and defense-grade ChiP modules; MIL-PRF-38534 compliant versions available; Extended temperature ranges; Enhanced screening and testing. Standard vs Hi-Rel: Standard modules for commercial/industrial use; Hi-Rel modules for aerospace, defense, medical; Custom screening available for specific requirements. Contact Vicor for high-reliability options.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for high-reliability ChiP module options and qualification support.

faqs.question: How do I assembly ChiP modules on PCBs?
faqs.answer: ChiP module assembly process: Standard SMT reflow process compatible; Follow Vicor assembly guidelines; Key steps: Apply solder paste to PCB pads; Place module using pick-and-place; Reflow solder following profile; Inspect for alignment and solder joints. Important considerations: Flatness of PCB substrate; Solder paste coverage under thermal pad; Reflow profile compatibility; No-wash requirement if thermal pad is grounded. Vicor provides detailed assembly instructions and reflow profiles.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for assembly guidelines and reflow profile recommendations.

products.name: 48V Input 1.3kW ChiP Non-Isolated Converter
products.shortDescription: 1.3kW high-efficiency non-isolated ChiP module with 48V input, ideal for high-density power applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The BCM48BF480T1K3A00 is a 1.3kW non-isolated ChiP module operating from 48V input.
Delivers high power density with peak efficiency up to 97% in compact 23mm SM-ChiP package.
Ideal for 48V data center applications and high-density power distribution systems.

faeReview.author: Robert Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Data Center
faeReview.content: The BCM48BF480T1K3A00 delivers impressive power density in a standard SMT package. I've designed power systems for blade servers where space is at a premium, and this module was the perfect fit. The 1.3kW from a 23mm module is remarkable, and the 97% efficiency means minimal heat generation. We used multiple modules in parallel for redundant 48V power shelves. The SMT assembly was straightforward - our contract manufacturer had no issues with the reflow profile. For high-density 48V applications, this module is outstanding.
faeReview.highlight: Exceptional power density for 48V blade server and high-density applications

faqs.question: What is the output current capability of BCM48BF480T1K3A00?
faqs.answer: BCM48BF480T1K3A00 current specifications: Output current: 27A continuous at 48V output; Peak output current: 35A for transient conditions; Power limit: 1.3kW continuous. For higher current: Parallel multiple modules with current sharing; Use appropriate bus bar sizing for combined current; Configure droop sharing for load balancing. Thermal consideration: Current derating may be required at high ambient temperature; Calculate power dissipation: P_loss = P_out x (1/η - 1); At 1.3kW, 97% efficiency: ~44W dissipation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for thermal derating guidance and parallel configuration recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I mount BCM48BF480T1K3A00 to a heatsink?
faqs.answer: Mounting procedure for BCM48BF480T1K3A00: Thermal interface: Apply thermal pad or grease to module thermal pad area; Heatsink surface: Ensure flatness < 0.1mm, clean surface; Mounting screws: M3 screws, 0.56-0.79 Nm torque; Use spring washers for consistent pressure. Assembly sequence: Apply TIM to module bottom; Place module on heatsink; Install and torque screws in cross pattern; Verify even TIM spread. Don't overtighten - can damage module case. Vicor provides detailed mounting instructions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for mounting guidelines and thermal interface recommendations.

faqs.question: Can I use BCM48BF480T1K3A00 for battery charging?
faqs.answer: Using BCM48BF480T1K3A00 for battery charging: Direct battery connection: Output is 48V fixed ratio, not regulated; For battery charging: Need additional regulation or use with PRM for controlled charging. Alternative approaches: Use PRM + VTM for regulated charging; Implement external current limiting; Add post-regulator for precise voltage control. For 48V battery packs: Can connect directly with proper battery management; May need pre-regulator for constant current charging. Vicor recommends consulting with FAE for battery charging applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for battery charging system design recommendations.

faqs.question: What EMI filtering is required for BCM48BF480T1K3A00?
faqs.answer: EMI considerations for BCM48BF480T1K3A00: Module meets EN 55032 Class B with external filter; Vicor recommends specific EMI filter components; External filter typically includes: X capacitors on input/output; Common-mode chokes; Y capacitors for isolation if needed. Filter design: Follow Vicor EMI filter guidelines; Account for cable length and system configuration; Pre-compliance testing recommended. Vicor provides filter schematics and component recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for EMI filter design and pre-compliance testing support.

faqs.question: What is the inrush current behavior?
faqs.answer: BCM48BF480T1K3A00 inrush current: Limited inrush current at startup; Controlled soft-start to limit input current surge; Output pre-bias capability - can start with voltage present on output. Startup sequence: Enable signal initiates turn-on; Controlled current ramp during soft-start; Full power available after startup complete. System design: Account for inrush when sizing input protection; Consider input fuse characteristics; Multiple modules may have cumulative inrush. Vicor provides startup current specifications for system design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for startup characteristics and system protection guidance.

Our distributor selection guide helps you choose the right power system configuration for your application requirements. All power systems leverage Vicor's modular approach, enabling flexible scaling and customization while maintaining proven reliability.

products.name: 48V 3kW Factorized Power System
products.shortDescription: Complete 3kW power system using FPA architecture with PRM and VTM modules for high-efficiency power delivery.
descriptionParagraphs: The PFM48AF480T016A00 is a complete 3kW power system based on Vicor's Factorized Power Architecture.
Combines PRM and VTM modules for high-efficiency 48V to point-of-load power delivery.
Pre-configured and tested for rapid deployment in data center and high-performance computing applications.
features: Complete FPA power system - plug and play

faeReview.author: Dr. James Chen
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Computing Power
faeReview.content: The PFM48AF480T016A00 is the answer for power-hungry AI accelerators. I've worked with several AI computing companies struggling with power delivery to GPUs and ASICs. This pre-configured FPA system delivers 3kW in a 1U form factor that fits standard racks. The PMBus monitoring is essential for power management in large deployments - we can track power consumption, detect problems early, and optimize power allocation across the system. The parallel capability allows scaling to megawatt levels for large AI training clusters. This system has enabled us to deploy unprecedented compute density.
faeReview.highlight: Enables unprecedented compute density for AI accelerator deployments
